refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task duration, material moved, and any safety/policy violations. Establish baseline and delta metrics.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide sufficient power, speed, and digging/loading capacity to move material at rates consistent with productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operators to control the Vehicle with latency, fidelity, and situational awareness sufficient to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services system shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without reducing productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry and task feedback to enable operator decision-making and productivity monitoring.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-power hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with advanced operator controls (on-site joystick + remote teleoperation interface) and real-time telemetry feedback to enable both direct and remote task execution at target productivity rates.

DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with swappable attachments (bucket, blade, ripper) and adaptive control algorithms that optimize task execution based on soil conditions and operator mode, coupled with a safety-gated tasking system.

DP-C: Implement a hybrid control architecture where the Vehicle executes pre-planned task sequences (autonomous or semi-autonomous) with operator override capability, supported by continuous telemetry and safety monitoring to maintain productivity while enforcing safety constraints.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate a high-power hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with advanced operator controls (on-site joystick + remote teleoperation interface) and real-time telemetry feedback to enable both direct and remote task execution at target productivity rates.

potential_sources: Vehicle power/hydraulic system couples to operator control responsiveness; poor hydraulic response degrades remote operator effectiveness.
Telemetry latency couples to operator situational awareness; high latency reduces productivity in remote mode.
Safety Services constraints couple to task execution speed; overly restrictive safety gates may reduce productivity below target.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A is minimally coupled at this level because it delegates specific control logic, safety enforcement, and telemetry architecture to sub-level DPs. The coupling risks are manageable through interface specification (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) and performance budgets (latency, bandwidth, control response time).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator skill and training are sufficient to achieve productivity_target in both on-site and remote modes. Risk: Inadequate operator training or fatigue may limit actual productivity regardless of vehicle capability.

Assumption: Network infrastructure (for Teleoperation) provides sufficient bandwidth and latency (<200ms round-trip) to support remote control. Risk: Poor network conditions at worksite may make remote operation infeasible.
Assumption: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are compatible with productivity_target. Risk: Overly conservative safety policies may make productivity_target unachievable.
Assumption: Hydraulic system response time is <500ms for operator commands. Risk: Slower response times degrade operator control fidelity, especially in remote mode.
Assumption: Telemetry data (camera, sensors, position) is sufficient for remote operator situational awareness. Risk: Blind spots or sensor failures may cause accidents or inefficiency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly specify performance budgets: (1) Vehicle control response time ≤500ms, (2) Teleoperation network latency ≤200ms round-trip, (3) Telemetry update rate ≥10Hz for critical sensors, (4) Safety Services shall not reduce productivity below 95% of vehicle capability. These budgets should be flowed down to sub-level DPs and verified during integration testing.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The top-level system integration boundary that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems to deliver end-to-end productivity capability. Owns the overall system architecture, interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link), and acceptance criteria verification.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time sensor data (vehicle position, orientation, hydraulic pressure, bucket load, camera feeds) streamed from Vehicle to Teleoperation and Safety Services for operator situational awareness and safety monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands (movement, digging, bucket control) received from on-site operator interface or Teleoperation system and routed to Vehicle control subsystem.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical alerts (proximity warnings, policy violations, emergency stop triggers) generated by Safety Services and communicated to operator and system controller.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional network connection supporting Teleoperation remote control and Telemetry data transmission; subject to latency and bandwidth constraints.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for this FR. It owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and is responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery. Minimal diffusion: only the acceptance criteria verification (measurement and reporting) may diffuse to external stakeholders (Site Manager, Project Manager).

Vehicle contains the core hydraulic power and digging capacity, which are necessary but not sufficient for productivity_target. Productivity also depends on operator control effectiveness (Teleoperation) and safety constraints (Safety Services). Assigning FR to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for operator interface quality and safety enforcement.

Teleoperation enables remote operator control, which is one mode of task execution. However, on-site operation is equally important, and productivity depends on Vehicle capability and Safety Services constraints. Assigning FR to Teleoperation alone would ignore on-site operator mode and vehicle limitations.

Safety Services enforces constraints that affect productivity, but does not directly deliver productivity capability. Assigning FR to Safety Services would invert the responsibility hierarchy: Safety Services should enable productivity within safety bounds, not own the productivity target itself.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because: (1) it owns the integration of all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) required to deliver productivity, (2) it defines and manages the interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) that couple subsystems, (3) it is responsible for end-to-end acceptance criteria verification (productivity measurement), and (4) it has clear ownership and accountability to stakeholders (Site Manager, Project Manager).
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration boundary; detailed productivity optimization logic may be unclear at this level. Mitigation: Decompose into sub-level FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) that assign specific responsibilities to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

Risk: Productivity measurement and reporting may require external tools or stakeholder involvement (Site Manager, Maintenance/Quality). Mitigation: Define clear measurement protocols and data collection interfaces as part of NGHE Solution acceptance criteria.

Risk: Productivity target may be unachievable due to worksite conditions, operator skill, or safety constraints. Mitigation: Establish baseline productivity metrics and delta analysis to identify limiting factors during trials.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution with explicit sub-FR allocation: FR-NGHE-001.1 to Vehicle, FR-NGHE-001.2 to Teleoperation, FR-NGHE-001.3 to Safety Services, FR-NGHE-001.4 to NGHE Solution (Telemetry/Tasking orchestration). Define interface contracts and performance budgets for each sub-FR to ensure coherent productivity delivery.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (integrated high-power vehicle with dual operator controls and real-time telemetry) directly addresses the refined FR (enable operator to execute tasks at productivity_target in both on-site and remote modes). The DP is sufficiently specific to guide sub-level design (Vehicle power/hydraulics, Teleoperation latency, Telemetry architecture) while remaining flexible enough to accommodate alternative implementations (e.g., different hydraulic systems, different teleoperation protocols). No independence axiom violations detected: the DP does not over-constrain the design space or introduce unnecessary coupling.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Telemetry architecture (sensor selection, data format, transmission protocol) may be split between Vehicle (sensor integration), Teleoperation (display/rendering), and Safety Services (alert generation). Mitigation: Define Telemetry interface contract at NGHE Solution level to ensure consistent data semantics and latency.

Diffusion Risk 2: Operator control responsiveness depends on Vehicle hydraulic response time and Teleoperation network latency. If these are not coordinated, the combined latency may exceed acceptable thresholds. Mitigation: Establish end-to-end latency budget (e.g., ≤500ms) and allocate to Vehicle (≤300ms) and Teleoperation (≤200ms).

Diffusion Risk 3: Safety Services constraints may conflict with productivity optimization. If Safety Services is overly conservative, it may reduce productivity below target. Mitigation: Involve Safety Manager in DP refinement to ensure safety policies are compatible with productivity_target.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for this DP because it owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and manages the interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) that couple them. The DP does not require new modules or interfaces beyond those already defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 is feasible through representative operations trials: (1) conduct earthmoving tasks under agreed conditions, (2) measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h), (3) compare against productivity_target, (4) log operator mode, task duration, material moved, and safety/policy compliance. Acceptance criteria are objective and measurable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear: NGHE Solution owns end-to-end productivity delivery and is responsible for integrating Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to achieve productivity_target. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) allocate specific responsibilities to subsystems. Ownership and accountability are unambiguous.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Establish performance budgets for DP-A sub-components: Vehicle control response time ≤500ms, Teleoperation network latency ≤200ms round-trip, Telemetry update rate ≥10Hz, Safety Services productivity impact ≤5%.
Revision 2: Define Telemetry interface contract (data types, update rates, latency requirements) at NGHE Solution level to ensure consistency across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

Revision 3: Involve Safety Manager in DP refinement to validate that worksite safety plan and operational policies are compatible with productivity_target. If not, negotiate revised safety policies or productivity target.
Revision 4: Establish baseline productivity metrics (on-site and remote modes) during early trials to identify limiting factors and inform sub-level design optimization.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into sub-level FRs to assign specific responsibilities to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, but further decomposition is necessary to guide detailed design and ensure coherent productivity delivery. The four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) are well-defined and ready for next-level decomposition.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide sufficient power, speed, and digging/loading capacity to move material at rates consistent with productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operators to control the Vehicle with latency, fidelity, and situational awareness sufficient to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services system shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without reducing productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry and task feedback to enable operator decision-making and productivity monitoring.

assumptions: Operator skill and training are sufficient to achieve productivity_target in both on-site and remote modes.
Network infrastructure provides sufficient bandwidth and latency (<200ms round-trip) to support remote control.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies are compatible with productivity_target.
Hydraulic system response time is <500ms for operator commands.
Telemetry data (camera, sensors, position) is sufficient for remote operator situational awareness.
Productivity_target is defined in m³/h or ton/h and is achievable with representative equipment and operators.
open_risks: Risk: Inadequate operator training or fatigue may limit actual productivity regardless of vehicle capability. Mitigation: Establish operator training program and fatigue management protocols.
Risk: Poor network conditions at worksite may make remote operation infeasible. Mitigation: Conduct network assessment at representative worksites and establish fallback to on-site operation.
Risk: Overly conservative safety policies may make productivity_target unachievable. Mitigation: Involve Safety Manager in DP refinement and negotiate compatible safety/productivity trade-offs.
Risk: Slower hydraulic response times degrade operator control fidelity, especially in remote mode. Mitigation: Establish control response time budget and validate during integration testing.
Risk: Blind spots or sensor failures may cause accidents or inefficiency. Mitigation: Conduct hazard analysis and define sensor redundancy/coverage requirements.
evidence_needed: Baseline productivity metrics from representative operations trials (on-site and remote modes).
Network latency and bandwidth measurements at representative worksites.
Vehicle hydraulic response time measurements under load.
Operator situational awareness assessment (camera coverage, sensor data quality).
Safety policy compatibility analysis (worksite safety plan vs. productivity_target).
Acceptance criteria verification plan (measurement protocols, data collection, reporting).
